questionText: Provide details of the policies which include environmental requirements that clients/investees need to meet.
questionKo: the policies which include environmental requirements that 고객/투자대상 need to meet.에 대한 세부 정보를 입력하십시오.
simpleHelp: To help manage environmental risks, organizations should integrate environmental issues into existing policy frameworks. These policies may apply across the organization and may be based on sectors, geographies, business lines, asset classes or other. Although the wave of environmental policies and regulations is growing, their implementation varies across organizations. This question helps data users understand which corporate policies integrate environmental issues, and what proportion of a financial services company's portfolio is covered by the policy.

options: Climate change
Water

guidance.text: This question only appears if you select "Yes, our policies include environmental requirements that clients/investees need to meet" or "Yes, our framework includes both policies with client/investee requirements and environmental exclusion policies" in response to column 1 "Policy framework for portfolio activities include environmental requirements for clients/investees, and/or exclusion policies" of 4.7.
guidance.textKo: This 문항 only appears 만약 you select "예, our policies include environmental requirements that 고객/투자대상 need to meet" 또는 "예, our framework includes both policies with 고객/투자대상 requirements 및 environmental exclusion policies" in response to 열 1 "정책 프레임워크 for 포트폴리오 activities include environmental requirements for 고객/투자대상, 및/또는 exclusion policies" of 4.7.

guidance.text:
General 
Add at least one row for each portfolio presented in column 1 "Portfolio".
If you have policies with different specifics in a single portfolio, add a separate row for each type of policy.
Portfolio (column 1) 
The options in this column appear dependent on the activities selected in 1.10.
Type of policy (column 3) 
The options in this column appear dependent on the activity indicated in column 1 "Portfolio" for the row.
Select all the policies into which environmental issues are integrated.
If there are policies within your organization's policy framework into which environmental issues are integrated, and which are not well represented by any of the drop-down options, select "Other, please specify" and provide a label.
Public availability (column 4) 
Indicate whether the selected policy is available to the general public or not.
Attach the policy (column 5) 
This column is optional if you select "Not publicly available" in column "Public availability".
Value chain stages of client/investee covered by policy (column 6) 
Indicate whether the requirement listed in column 13 "Requirements for clients/investees" is applicable to clients/investees operational activities only, or whether the requirement must also be fulfilled in other parts of the value chain.
Industry sectors covered by the policy (column 7) 
Indicate the industry sectors the requirements apply to, as stated in the written policy.
If it is stated in the written policy that the requirement should be applied to all industry sectors, indicate this by selecting "Other, please specify" and writing "all sectors".
Commodities covered by the policy (column 8) 
Indicate the commodities that the requirements apply to, as stated in the written policy.
You are able to select "All agricultural commodities" if either this coverage is stated explicitly in the policy or if the commodities covered by the policy encompass all agricultural commodities you have exposure to within your portfolio.
Commodities in this question could include:
Timber products: this includes all forms of solid timber, including logs and other wood fiber, processed forms of timber (e.g., boards or pulp), and wood by-products (e.g., sawdust). Please note, this also includes products made from wood fiber such as paper, packaging, cardboard and specialty fibers, e.g., viscose, as well as, timber products used in biofuel production.
Palm oil: this includes all forms of palm oil, including palm nuts and kernels, processed forms of palm oil (e.g., crude palm oil or palm kernel oil), palm oil by-products (e.g., oilcake or glycerol), and any of their derivatives. Please note, this also includes palm oil used as ingredients in products (such as food, household, or animal feed products), as well as palm oil used in biofuel production.
Cattle products: this includes all forms of cattle products, including beef, processed forms of cattle (e.g., leather), cattle by-products (e.g., glycerin, gelatin or tallow), and all other products derived from cattle. Please note, this also includes cattle products used as ingredients in other products (such as food, household and apparel products), as well as tallow used in biofuel production. Dairy products are not included in this category.
Soy: this includes all forms of soy, including whole soybean, processed forms of soy (e.g., soybean oil, meal, or flour), soy by-products (e.g., oilcake or soy lecithin) and any of their derivatives. Please note, this also includes soy used as ingredients in products (such as food, household, or animal feed products), as well as soy used in biofuel production. In addition, soy which has been embedded as animal feed in animal products e.g., meat, farmed fish, dairy, eggs, or other animal products as ingredients is included in this category.
Rubber: this includes all forms of natural rubber, including, processed forms of rubber (vulcanized and unvulcanized forms), and all other products derived from rubber. Please note, this also includes rubber used in products such as tires, conveyer belts, tubes, and apparel.
Cocoa: this includes all forms of cocoa, including cocoa beans (raw and roasted), cocoa by-products (e.g. cocoa husks), processed forms of cocoa (e.g. paste, powder or butter), and all other products derived from cocoa. Please note, this also includes cocoa used as ingredients in food products (such as food, household, or animal feed products).
Coffee : this includes all forms of coffee, including coffee beans (raw and roasted), coffee by-products (e.g. coffee husks), processed forms of coffee (e.g. coffee contained within coffee substitutes), and all other products derived from coffee. Please note, this also includes coffee used as ingredients in products (such as food or household products).
Commodity value chain stage covered by the policy (column 9) 
Indicate the commodity value chain stage of the client/investee that is covered by the policy. For example, a bank disclosing a lending policy that specifies requirements for the direct operations of its client, who is a timber producer, should disclose "Direct operations" in column "Value chain stages of client/investee covered by policy") and "Production" in the column "Commodity value chain stage covered by the policy".
% of portfolio covered by the policy in relation to total portfolio value (column 10) 
Enter the percentage of portfolio value that has been covered by the policy in relation to the portfolio value based on the assets value reported in 1.10.
Coverage by portfolio value can be based on either total or outstanding commitments, premiums, committed capital, and/or other.
Basis of exceptions to policy (column 11) 
This column appears if you enter any value lower than 100% in column 10 "% of portfolio covered by the policy in relation to total portfolio value".
If the policy does not apply to all of your portfolio, indicate the basis for the exceptions. For example, if the policy only applies to transactions above $10m, select "Transaction size".
Explain how criteria coverage and/or exceptions have been determined (column 12) 
Explain how you calculated the percentage of portfolio coverage in column 10 "% of portfolio covered by the policy in relation to total portfolio value".
Provide details of the exception criteria you selected in column 11 and explain why you chose these specific exceptions, if applicable.
Requirements for clients/investees (column 13) 
Select all the requirements for your clients/investees, indicate e.g., if you require your clients/investees to commit to a circular economy strategy.
Requirements can refer to actions that must be taken, or to other possible criteria which clients/investees must fulfil.
Net-Zero Banking Alliance (NZBA) members disclosing on their NZBA commitment on "engaging on corporate and industry (financial and real economy) action, as well as public policies, to help support a net-zero transition of economic sectors in line with science and giving consideration to associated social impacts" should select "Commitment to net-zero emissions".
Select "Other, please specify" to add any requirement not listed.
Measurement of proportion of clients/investees compliant with the policy (column 14) 
For each portfolio covered by environmental requirements, select if you are able to provide data on the clients/investees compliant with your requirements.
If you have environmental requirements for clients/investees but you do not record compliance against the requirements, but you plan to within the next two years, select "No, but we plan to measure this within the next two years".
If you have environmental requirements for clients/investees but you do not record compliance against the requirements, and do not plan to within the next two years, select "No, and we do not plan to measure this in the next two years".
% of clients/investees compliant with the policy (column 15) 
Provide a percentage value for the number of clients/investees that meet all your requirements, in relation to the total number of clients/investees within the portfolio.
% of portfolio value that is compliant with the policy (column 16) 
Provide a percentage value for the portfolio exposure to clients/investees that meet all your requirements, in relation to the total exposure to all clients/investees within the portfolio.
Target year for 100% compliance (column 17) 
For each row, select the target timeframe for achieving 100% compliance of the portfolio with the reported requirement/s.
If you have set a target to achieve 100% compliance, but do not have a specific deadline, select "No timeframe".
If you do not plan on achieving 100% compliance, select "We have not set a target for 100% compliance".
Explain why your organization does not measure the % of clients/investees compliant with the policy (column 18) 
If you have not provided percentages for clients/investees compliant with environmental requirements, explain why here.
If applicable, give details of your organization's plans to measure compliance with environmental requirements in the future.
guidance.textKo: 일반 Add 최소 one 행 for each 포트폴리오 presented in 열 1 "포트폴리오". 만약 you have policies with different specifics in a single 포트폴리오, add a separate 행 for each type of 정책. 포트폴리오 (열 1) The options in this 열 appear dependent on the activities 선택 in 1.10. Type of 정책 (열 3) The options in this 열 appear dependent on the activity indicated in 열 1 "포트폴리오" 에 대한 행. Select all the policies into which 환경 이슈 are integrated. 만약 there are policies within 귀사의 정책 프레임워크 into which 환경 이슈 are integrated, 및 which are not well represented by 하나 이상 의 drop-down options, select "Other, 직접 입력" 및 provide a label. 공개 availability (열 4) Indicate whether the 선택 정책 is available to the general 공개 또는 not. Attach the 정책 (열 5) This 열 is optional 만약 you select "Not 공개되어 있음" in 열 "공개 availability". 가치사슬 stages of 고객/투자대상 covered by 정책 (열 6) Indicate whether the requirement listed in 열 13 "Requirements for 고객/투자대상" is 해당되는 경우 to 고객/투자대상 operational activities only, 또는 whether the requirement 해야 합니다 also be fulfilled in other parts 의 가치사슬. Industry sectors covered by the 정책 (열 7) Indicate the industry sectors the requirements apply to, as stated in the written 정책. 만약 it is stated in the written 정책 that the requirement 해야 합니다 be applied to all industry sectors, indicate this by selecting "Other, 직접 입력" 및 writing "전 섹터". Commodities covered by the 정책 (열 8) Indicate the commodities that the requirements apply to, as stated in the written 정책. You are able to select "All 농산물 원자재" 만약 either this coverage is stated explicitly in the 정책 또는 만약 the commodities covered by the 정책 encompass all 농산물 원자재 you have exposure to within your 포트폴리오. Commodities in this 문항 could include: Timber products: this includes all forms of solid timber, 포함 logs 및 other wood fiber, 프로세스ed forms of timber (e.g., 이사회s 또는 pulp), 및 wood by-products (e.g., sawdust). Please note, this also includes products made from wood fiber such as paper, packaging, card이사회 및 specialty fibers, e.g., viscose, as well as, timber products used in biofuel production. Palm oil: this includes all forms of palm oil, 포함 palm nuts 및 kernels, 프로세스ed forms of palm oil (e.g., crude palm oil 또는 palm kernel oil), palm oil by-products (e.g., oilcake 또는 glycerol), 및 하나 이상 의ir derivatives. Please note, this also includes palm oil used as ingredients in products (such as food, household, 또는 animal feed products), as well as palm oil used in biofuel production. Cattle products: this includes all forms of cattle products, 포함 beef, 프로세스ed forms of cattle (e.g., leather), cattle by-products (e.g., glycerin, gelatin 또는 tallow), 및 all other products derived from cattle. Please note, this also includes cattle products used as ingredients in other products (such as food, household 및 apparel products), as well as tallow used in biofuel production. Dairy products are not included in this category. Soy: this includes all forms of soy, 포함 whole soybean, 프로세스ed forms of soy (e.g., soybean oil, meal, 또는 flour), soy by-products (e.g., oilcake 또는 soy lecithin) 및 하나 이상 의ir derivatives. Please note, this also includes soy used as ingredients in products (such as food, household, 또는 animal feed products), as well as soy used in biofuel production. In addition, soy which has been embedded as animal feed in animal products e.g., meat, farmed fish, dairy, eggs, 또는 other animal products as ingredients is included in this category. Rubber: this includes all forms of natural rubber, 포함, 프로세스ed forms of rubber (vulcanized 및 unvulcanized forms), 및 all other products derived from rubber. Please note, this also includes rubber used in products such as tires, conveyer belts, tubes, 및 apparel. Cocoa: this includes all forms of cocoa, 포함 cocoa beans (raw 및 roasted), cocoa by-products (e.g. cocoa husks), 프로세스ed forms of cocoa (e.g. paste, powder 또는 butter), 및 all other products derived from cocoa. Please note, this also includes cocoa used as ingredients in food products (such as food, household, 또는 animal feed products). Coffee: this includes all forms of coffee, 포함 coffee beans (raw 및 roasted), coffee by-products (e.g. coffee husks), 프로세스ed forms of coffee (e.g. coffee contained within coffee substitutes), 및 all other products derived from coffee. Please note, this also includes coffee used as ingredients in products (such as food 또는 household products). Commodity 가치사슬 stage covered by the 정책 (열 9) Indicate the commodity 가치사슬 stage 의 고객/투자대상 that is covered by the 정책. For example, a bank disclosing a lending 정책 that specifies requirements 에 대한 직접 운영사업장 of its client, who is a timber producer, 해야 합니다 disclose "직접 운영사업장" in 열 "가치사슬 stages of 고객/투자대상 covered by 정책") 및 "Production" in the 열 "Commodity 가치사슬 stage covered by the 정책". % of 포트폴리오 covered by the 정책 in relation to total 포트폴리오 value (열 10) Enter the percentage of 포트폴리오 value that has been covered by the 정책 in relation to the 포트폴리오 value based on the 자산 value 보고 in 1.10. Coverage by 포트폴리오 value can be based on either total 또는 outstanding commitments, premiums, committed capital, 및/또는 other. Basis of exceptions to 정책 (열 11) This 열 appears 만약 you enter 하나 이상 value lower than 100% in 열 10 "% of 포트폴리오 covered by the 정책 in relation to total 포트폴리오 value". 만약 the 정책 does not apply to all of your 포트폴리오, indicate the basis 에 대한 exceptions. For example, 만약 the 정책 only applies to transactions above $10m, select "Transaction size". Explain how 기준 coverage 및/또는 exceptions have been determined (열 12) Explain how you calculated the percentage of 포트폴리오 coverage in 열 10 "% of 포트폴리오 covered by the 정책 in relation to total 포트폴리오 value". 세부 정보를 입력 의 exception 기준 you 선택 in 열 11 및 그 이유를 설명 you chose these specific exceptions, 만약 해당되는 경우. Requirements for 고객/투자대상 (열 13) Select all the requirements 귀사의 고객/투자대상, indicate e.g., 만약 you require your 고객/투자대상 to commit to a circular economy 전략. Requirements can refer to actions that 해야 합니다 be taken, 또는 to other possible 기준 which 고객/투자대상 해야 합니다 fulfil. Net-Zero Banking Alliance (NZBA) members disclosing on their NZBA commitment on "engaging on corporate 및 industry (financial 및 real economy) action, as well as 공개 policies, to help support a net-zero transition of economic sectors in line with science 및 giving consideration to associated social impacts" 해야 합니다 select "Commitment to net-zero emissions". Select "Other, 직접 입력" to add 하나 이상 requirement not listed. Measurement of proportion of 고객/투자대상 compliant with the 정책 (열 14) For each 포트폴리오 covered by environmental requirements, select 만약 you are able to provide data on the 고객/투자대상 compliant with your requirements. 만약 you have environmental requirements for 고객/투자대상 but you do not record compliance against the requirements, but you plan to within the next two years, select "No, but we plan to measure this within the next two years". 만약 you have environmental requirements for 고객/투자대상 but you do not record compliance against the requirements, 및 do not plan to within the next two years, select "No, 및 we do not plan to measure this in the next two years". % of 고객/투자대상 compliant with the 정책 (열 15) Provide a percentage value 에 대한 number of 고객/투자대상 that meet all your requirements, in relation to the total number of 고객/투자대상 within the 포트폴리오. % of 포트폴리오 value that is compliant with the 정책 (열 16) Provide a percentage value 에 대한 포트폴리오 exposure to 고객/투자대상 that meet all your requirements, in relation to the total exposure to all 고객/투자대상 within the 포트폴리오. 목표 year for 100% compliance (열 17) For each 행, select the 목표 timeframe for achieving 100% compliance 의 포트폴리오 with the 보고 requirement/s. 만약 you have set a 목표 to achieve 100% compliance, but do not have a specific deadline, select "No timeframe". 만약 you do not plan on achieving 100% compliance, select "We have not set a 목표 for 100% compliance". 그 이유를 설명 귀사 does not measure the % of 고객/투자대상 compliant with the 정책 (열 18) 만약 you have not 입력 percentages for 고객/투자대상 compliant with environmental requirements, 그 이유를 설명 here. 만약 해당되는 경우, give details of 귀사의 plans to measure compliance with environmental requirements in the future.

guidance.text:
The organization has publicly available policies which address environmental dependencies, impacts, risks, and opportunities related to the organization and its value chain.
The policies apply organization-wide, and to the organization's value chain where relevant.
The organization specifies whether and how its policies are linked to absolute and interim timebound, quantifiable and measurable targets that include reference dates/cut-off dates/boundaries/coverage/exclusions.
Financial institutions have policies which include environmental requirements that clients/investees need to meet.
guidance.textKo: The organization has 공개되어 있음 policies which address 환경 의존성, 임팩트, 리스크 및 기회 related to the organization 및 its 가치사슬. The policies apply organization-wide, 및 to the 조직의 가치사슬 여기서 관련. The organization specifies whether 및 how its policies are linked to absolute 및 interim timebound, quantifiable 및 measurable 목표 that include reference dates/cut-off dates/boundaries/coverage/제외사항. Financial institutions have policies which include environmental requirements that 고객/투자대상 need to meet.

guidance.text:
Forests policy: a statement by a portfolio organization specifying the goals or targets that it intends to meet or the actions it intends to take regarding its management of forest-related issues. 
Landscape: defined geographic area with common and interacting ecological and socioeconomic characteristics. They may be delineated based on river basins, seascapes, ecosystems, jurisdictional boundaries, or in other ways (adapted from AFi, 2024 ). ​ 
Landscape and jurisdictional initiative:  an on-the-ground collaborative program to set common goals, take collective action while reconciling different interests, and monitor progress towards improving social, environmental, and economic outcomes at a landscape/jurisdictional scale. 
Manufacturing: the series of actions, methods, and techniques that transform raw or processed materials into final products ready for human use/consumption. 
In palm oil and soybean value chains, this stage may include the refining of oil into shortening and the use of ingredients in the manufacture of bakery products. 
For timber products, the manufacturing stage involves the secondary processing of wood into multiple products. This may include furniture, flooring, plywood and boards, as well as other building materials. For pulp and paper, this may include the conversion of pulp to printing and writing paper, newsprint, tissue, container boards, and packaging. 
Organizations may manufacture cattle products into products containing beef (e.g., retail meat products, fast food, byproducts including glycerin/gelatin) and leather products for various industries (e.g., footwear, clothing, furniture and car upholstery) (adapted from Global Canopy, 2018 and AFi, 2024 ). 
Portfolio:  in the context of this questionnaire your portfolio is your entire collection of your core financing activities and insurance policies that you offer. For banking, this is the entire collection of products, securities and loans held on your balance sheet for which you own the receivable stream. For asset managers, this is the entire collection of your products and investments that you hold and/or manage on behalf of your clients. For asset owners, this is the entire collection of products, funds and investments owned and controlled by your company. For investment portfolios, asset managers should consider discretionary investments, those where the company has discretion over investment decision. For insurance underwriting, this is the entire collection of products and insurance policies you provide to your clients 
Processing: the series of actions that transform raw materials from their natural state to an initial processed state in preparation for market or further processing/manufacturing. 
Organizations involved in the production of crude palm oil from crushed fruit or the production of soybean oil and soybean meal from soybean would select this activity. Further processing activities in the form of refining and fractionation should be considered in the manufacturing stage. 
In the case of timber products, organizations whose activities include the initial processing of timber products in mills (i.e., sawmills, plywood and veneer mills, pulp, and paper mills) would select this stage. 
For cattle products, these activities primarily involve the slaughtering of cattle and the processing of raw hide into leather (adapted from Global Canopy, 2018 and AFi, 2024 ). 
Production: the first stage of the upstream value chain, often entailing the production of raw agricultural and forest products by farm owners, smallholders, and communities. Vertically integrated companies are also involved in producing activities if they own or manage land used for production (adapted from Global Canopy, 2018 and AFi, 2024 ). 
Retailing: selling products directly to individual consumers. This includes supermarkets, convenience stores, lumber and home improvement stores, home furnishing stores, online retailers, and restaurant chains (adapted from Global Canopy, 2018 and AFi, 2024 ). 
Trading: purchasing and selling raw or primary processed agricultural or forestry materials to domestic or export markets. This includes shipments, transport, and storage of the commodities (adapted from Global Canopy, 2018 and AFi, 2024 ). 
Zero‐carbon‐ready building: a building that is highly energy efficient based on local rating schemes and uses either renewable energy directly or an energy supply that will be fully decarbonized by 2050, such as electricity or district heat ( SBTi, adapted from IEA, 2025 ).
guidance.textKo: Forests 정책: a statement by a 포트폴리오 organization specifying the goals 또는 목표 that it intends to meet 또는 the actions it intends to take regarding its 관리 of forest-related issues. Landscape: defined geographic area with common 및 interacting ecological 및 socioeconomic characteristics. They may be delineated based on river basins, seascapes, ecosystems, jurisdictional boundaries, 또는 in other ways (adapted from AFi, 2024). ​ Landscape 및 jurisdictional 이니셔티브: an on-the-ground collaborative program to set common goals, take collective action while reconciling different interests, 및 monitor progress towards improving social, environmental, 및 economic outcomes at a landscape/jurisdictional scale. Manufacturing: the series of actions, methods, 및 techniques that transform raw 또는 프로세스ed materials into final products ready for human use/consumption. In palm oil 및 soybean 가치사슬s, this stage may include the refining of oil into shortening 및 the use of ingredients in the manufacture of bakery products. For timber products, the manufacturing stage involves the secondary 프로세스ing of wood into multiple products. This may include furniture, flooring, plywood 및 이사회s, as well as other building materials. For pulp 및 paper, this may include the conversion of pulp to printing 및 writing paper, 신규sprint, tissue, container 이사회s, 및 packaging. Organizations may manufacture cattle products into products containing beef (e.g., retail meat products, fast food, byproducts 포함 glycerin/gelatin) 및 leather products for various industries (e.g., footwear, clothing, furniture 및 car upholstery) (adapted from Global Canopy, 2018 및 AFi, 2024). 포트폴리오: in the context of this 질문서 your 포트폴리오 is your entire collection of your core financing activities 및 insurance policies that you offer. For banking, this is the entire collection of products, securities 및 loans held on your balance sheet for which you own the receivable stream. For 자산운용사, this is the entire collection of your products 및 investments that you hold 및/또는 manage on behalf of your clients. For asset owners, this is the entire collection of products, funds 및 investments owned 및 controlled by your company. For investment 포트폴리오s, 자산운용사 해야 합니다 consider discretionary investments, those 여기서 the company has discretion over investment decision. For insurance underwriting, this is the entire collection of products 및 insurance policies you provide to your clients 프로세스ing: the series of actions that transform raw materials from their natural state to an initial 프로세스ed state in preparation for market 또는 further 프로세스ing/manufacturing. Organizations involved in the production of crude palm oil from crushed fruit 또는 the production of soybean oil 및 soybean meal from soybean would select this activity. Further 프로세스ing activities in the form of refining 및 fractionation 해야 합니다 be considered in the manufacturing stage. In the case of timber products, organizations whose activities include the initial 프로세스ing of timber products in mills (i.e., sawmills, plywood 및 veneer mills, pulp, 및 paper mills) would select this stage. For cattle products, these activities primarily involve the slaughtering of cattle 및 the 프로세스ing of raw hide into leather (adapted from Global Canopy, 2018 및 AFi, 2024). Production: the first stage 의 업스트림 가치사슬, often entailing the production of raw agricultural 및 forest products by farm owners, smallholders, 및 communities. Vertically integrated companies are also involved in producing activities 만약 they own 또는 manage land used for production (adapted from Global Canopy, 2018 및 AFi, 2024). Retailing: selling products directly to individual consumers. This includes super시장, convenience stores, lumber 및 home improvement stores, home furnishing stores, online retailers, 및 restaurant chains (adapted from Global Canopy, 2018 및 AFi, 2024). Trading: purchasing 및 selling raw 또는 primary 프로세스ed agricultural 또는 forestry materials to domestic 또는 export 시장. This includes shipments, transport, 및 storage 의 commodities (adapted from Global Canopy, 2018 및 AFi, 2024). Zero‐carbon‐ready building: a building that is highly energy efficient based on local rating schemes 및 uses either 재생에너지 directly 또는 an energy supply that will be fully decarbonized by 2050, such as electricity 또는 district heat (SBTi, adapted from IEA, 2025).

criteria.criteria:
ROUTE A)
For rows where 'Climate change' is selected in column 'Environmental issues covered' AND
'Publicly available' selected in column 'Public availability' OR column 'Public availability' is not completed:
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of cells displayed.
A maximum of 4/4 points is available for this route.
OR
ROUTE B)
For rows where 'Climate change' is selected in column 'Environmental issues covered' AND
'Not publicly available' selected in column 'Public availability':
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of cells displayed (excluding column 'Attach the policy').
A maximum of 3.5/3.5 points is available for this route.
OR
NOT APPLICABLE ROUTE)
'Climate change' NOT selected in column 'Environmental issues covered' in any row - 0/0 points
A maximum of 0/0 points is available for this route.
One row scored.
criteria.criteriaKo: 경로 A) For 행 여기서 '기후변화' is 선택 in 열 '환경 이슈 covered' 그리고 '공개되어 있음' 선택 in 열 '공개 availability' 또는 열 '공개 availability' is not 완성: 표시된 칸(셀)의 개수에 비례하여 완성된 칸(셀)마다 점수가 부여됩니다. 이 경로에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 4/4점입니다. 또는 경로 B) For 행 여기서 '기후변화' is 선택 in 열 '환경 이슈 covered' 그리고 'Not 공개되어 있음' 선택 in 열 '공개 availability': 점수가 부여됩니다 per 완성된 칸(셀) in proportion to the number of 표시된 칸(셀) (제외 열 'Attach the 정책'). A 최고 of 3.5/3.5 점 is available for this 경로. 또는 NOT APPLICABLE 경로) '기후변화' NOT 선택 in 열 '환경 이슈 covered' in 하나 이상 행 - 0/0 점 이 경로에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 0/0점입니다. One 행 scored.

criteria.criteria:
The following conditions must be met to be eligible for Awareness points:
- Full Disclosure points awarded
- 'Climate change' selected in column 'Environmental issues covered' in at least one row
For rows where 'Climate change' is selected in column 'Environmental issues covered':
i) 'Publicly available' selected in column 'Public availability' - 0.5 points
ii) Figure greater than 0 provided in column '% of portfolio covered by the policy…' - 0.5 points
iii) Any option excluding any 'Other, please specify' option selected in column 'Requirements for clients/investees' - 0.5 points
iv) 'Yes' selected in column 'Measurement of proportion of clients/investees compliant with the policy' - 0.5 points
OR
'No, but we plan to measure this within the next two years' selected in column 'Measurement of proportion of clients/investees compliant with the policy' - 0.25/0.5 points
A maximum of 2/2 points is available for this question.
One row scored.
criteria.criteriaKo: The following conditions 해야 합니다 be met to be 획득 가능 for 인지 점: - 공시에서 최고점 획득 - '기후변화' 선택 in 열 '환경 이슈 covered' in 최소 one 행 For 행 여기서 '기후변화' is 선택 in 열 '환경 이슈 covered': i) '공개되어 있음' 선택 in 열 '공개 availability' - 0.5 점 ii) Figure 초과 0 입력 in 열 '% of 포트폴리오 covered by the 정책…' - 0.5 점 iii) 하나 이상 option 제외 하나 이상 'Other, 직접 입력' option 선택 in 열 'Requirements for 고객/투자대상' - 0.5 점 iv) '예' 선택 in 열 'Measurement of proportion of 고객/투자대상 compliant with the 정책' - 0.5 점 또는 'No, but we plan to measure this within the next two years' 선택 in 열 'Measurement of proportion of 고객/투자대상 compliant with the 정책' - 0.25/0.5 점 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 2/2점입니다. One 행 scored.

criteria.criteria:
The following conditions must be met to be eligible for Management points:
- Full Awareness points awarded
- 'Climate change' is selected in column 'Environmental issues covered' in at least one row
For rows where 'Climate change' is selected in column 'Environmental issues covered':
i) Both 'Commitment to disclose Scope 1 emissions' AND 'Commitment to disclose Scope 2 emissions' selected in column 'Requirements for clients/investees' AND
Either 'Commitment to disclose Scope 3 emissions' OR 'Commitment to develop a climate transition plan' selected in column 'Requirements for clients/investees' - 2 points
ii) Figure greater than 0 provided in either column '% of clients/investees compliant with the policy' OR column '% of portfolio value that is compliant with the policy' - 1 point
iii) Any option excluding both 'No timeframe' AND 'We have not set a target for 100% compliance' selected in column 'Target year for 100% compliance' - 1 point
A maximum of 4/4 points is available for this question.
One row scored.
criteria.criteriaKo: The following conditions 해야 합니다 be met to be 획득 가능 for 관리 점: - 인지에서 최고점 획득 - '기후변화' is 선택 in 열 '환경 이슈 covered' in 최소 one 행 For 행 여기서 '기후변화' is 선택 in 열 '환경 이슈 covered': i) Both 'Commitment to disclose Scope 1 배출량' 그리고 'Commitment to disclose Scope 2 배출량' 선택 in 열 'Requirements for 고객/투자대상' 그리고 Either 'Commitment to disclose Scope 3 배출량' 또는 'Commitment to develop a 기후 전환계획' 선택 in 열 'Requirements for 고객/투자대상' - 2 점 ii) Figure 초과 0 입력 in either 열 '% of 고객/투자대상 compliant with the 정책' 또는 열 '% of 포트폴리오 value that is compliant with the 정책' - 1 점 iii) 하나 이상 option 제외 both 'No timeframe' 그리고 'We have not set a 목표 for 100% compliance' 선택 in 열 '목표 year for 100% compliance' - 1 점 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 4/4점입니다. One 행 scored.

criteria.criteria:
The following conditions must be met to be eligible for Leadership points:
- Full Management points awarded
- 'Climate change' selected in column 'Environmental issues covered' in at least one row
For rows where 'Climate change' is selected in column 'Environmental issues covered':
Three or more of the following options are selected in column 'Requirements for clients/investees' - 2 points
- 'Commitment to 100% renewable energy'
- 'Commitment to net-zero emissions'
- 'Commitment to not funding climate-denial or lobbying against climate regulations'
- 'Commitment to not invest in fossil-fuel expansion'
- 'Commitment to set a science-based emissions reduction target'
- 'Commitment to stakeholder engagement and capacity building on environmental issues'
A maximum of 2/2 points is available for this question.
One row scored.
criteria.criteriaKo: The following conditions 해야 합니다 be met to be 획득 가능 for 리더십 점: - 관리에서 최고점 획득 - '기후변화' 선택 in 열 '환경 이슈 covered' in 최소 one 행 For 행 여기서 '기후변화' is 선택 in 열 '환경 이슈 covered': Three 또는 more 의 following options are 선택 in 열 'Requirements for 고객/투자대상' - 2 점 - 'Commitment to 100% 재생에너지' - 'Commitment to net-zero emissions' - 'Commitment to not funding climate-denial 또는 lobbying against climate regulations' - 'Commitment to not invest in fossil-fuel expansion' - 'Commitment to set a science-based emissions reduction 목표' - 'Commitment to 이해관계자 참여 및 capacity building on 환경 이슈' 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 2/2점입니다. One 행 scored.

simpleHelpKo: To help manage environmental 리스크, organizations 해야 합니다 integrate 환경 이슈 into existing 정책 프레임워크s. These policies may apply across the organization 및 may be based on sectors, geographies, business lines, asset classes 또는 other. Although the wave of environmental policies 및 regulations is g행ing, their 이행 varies across organizations. This 문항 helps 데이터 사용자 understand which corporate policies integrate 환경 이슈, 및 what proportion of a 금융 서비스 company's 포트폴리오 is covered by the 정책.
